task_completion.description: Verify the C# Console App project named 'SystemDiagnostic' is created in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File explorer, terminal, or IDE showing a folder or solution named 'SystemDiagnostic' located in 'C:\Users\Docker\source\repos'.

task_completion.description: Verify the C# code implements the required P/Invoke functions from kernel32.dll.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing C# source code with [DllImport("kernel32.dll")] and declarations for GetComputerNameW, GetSystemDirectoryW, GetCurrentProcessId, and GetTickCount64.

task_completion.description: Verify the C# code contains logic to format the report and write it to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing string formatting matching the required keys (ComputerName, SystemDirectory, ProcessId, UptimeMilliseconds) and file writing logic targeting 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\DiagnosticOutput\sysinfo_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: Verify the solution is built in 'Release' configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: IDE build configuration dropdown set to 'Release', terminal showing a 'dotnet build -c Release' command, or a 'bin\Release' folder visible in the project directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the diagnostic output directory and report file were successfully created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File explorer or terminal showing the file 'sysinfo_report.txt' exists inside 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\DiagnosticOutput'.

task_completion.description: Verify the generated report file contains the correct format and system data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of 'sysinfo_report.txt' with exactly four lines: 'ComputerName: [...]', 'SystemDirectory: [...]', 'ProcessId: [...]', and 'UptimeMilliseconds: [...]' populated with actual system values.

integrity.description: Ensure the output file contains realistic dynamic system data, not hardcoded placeholders.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values in 'sysinfo_report.txt' should look like real system data (e.g., SystemDirectory is likely 'C:\Windows\System32', ProcessId is a valid integer, UptimeMilliseconds is a large integer), not literal strings like '[YourPID]'.

integrity.description: Ensure the C# code is genuinely responsible for generating the output file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visible C# code should logically match the generated output file, without evidence of a separate script (like a Python or PowerShell script) being used to fake the text file creation.

integrity.description: Ensure the application was actually executed to generate the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution command (e.g., 'dotnet run' or running the compiled .exe) or the IDE showing a successful run/debug session.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Project Name: SystemDiagnostic. Expected Output Directory: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\DiagnosticOutput. Expected Output File: sysinfo_report.txt. The C# code must use P/Invoke for GetComputerNameW, GetSystemDirectoryW, GetCurrentProcessId, and GetTickCount64 from kernel32.dll. The output file must exactly match the format: 'ComputerName: [val]
SystemDirectory: [val]
ProcessId: [val]
UptimeMilliseconds: [val]'.
